payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I still haven't seen how the layout looks. I'm really excited for it. Also, for the guys who didn't notice, we've updated some of the models and we have colors and a new camera setup. Hopefully we're going to be testing it out in the weeks to come for camera angles and stuff. Things might shift as it happens, but the color scheme on each of the characters' bases corresponds to their portrait color, so it should be easier to distinguish who is who on the battle mat going forward, so hopefully that'll be helpful. All right! Let's get into the thick of it. So last we left off, Vox Machina had finished an extensive, dangerous, tension-filled adventure to the city of Whitestone, the historical landscape that Percival had grown up in, to take down the Briarwoods, two husband-and-wife team who had been responsible for ruling it with an iron fist for five years, doing a series of atrocities and seemingly preparing a ziggurat, or an ancient temple, beneath the city for some unspoken deity that the party then discovered to be an individual referred to as Vecna. They defeated the Briarwoods, managed to help the rebellion of the people of Whitestone retake their town, and helped them set up, prepare, and have their first Winter's Crest festival in five years, since they were taken by the Briarwoods. There was much game-playing and eating contests and polymorphed bears and very fun adventures and exchange of gifts and some fun moments and the party, as we return now, the festival over, a few days past, begin to put their affairs in order to return back to Emon in what their next order of business is. So picking up from there, it's been a few days since the Winter's Crest festival has finished, and most of the decorations, as quickly put together as they were given the circumstances, still loosely hang around the city as a reminder of the first real beacon of positivity in this city's history in a while. The town's much more lively, as people are less afraid to walk out into the streets during the day and it's like watching it really come to life over these past few weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for running with that.

preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh, it's so pretty! The panels behind us. I was just admiring--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, so over the past few days. Okay. As you guys are all preparing the circumstance over these next few days, you've gone into your original workshop, which is a little more ramshackle than the one you have back in Emon that was built for you. This was just the one that you had pieced together while you were growing up in Whitestone. I would like for you to go ahead and make a tinkering check, if you don't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Maybe we can wait until we get back to Emon and have Gilmore do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll talk to them and see what's possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we can't really ask Allura to do it. We stole the carpet from her in the first place. That would be a very bad move, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we could just say somebody stole it from her. We stole it from them, we were trying to return it, it fell in acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But then what? We wouldn't get it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, she fixes it. Oh, here's a gift for recovering the rug. It makes perfect sense to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, she was just using it as a rug.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that seems like a really expensive rug.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe she was keeping it secret. Keeping it safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, forget it. Let's just go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to craft that thing that I've been wanting to craft, so that was, that we talked about.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. (sighs) You manage to pull apart, over the period of a few days, Ripley's pistol. You take aspects of whitestone and residuum dust as well as residuum glass and begin to reconstruct it, based on its elements, in a much more refined, in a much more Percival-practiced method. You attach the fifth barrel, the one that you intend to be used to deliver short-range explosives, and it seems to fit together.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Plus, we had them send word to Uriel, remember? We had them send a letter to Uriel, explaining all the wonderful things we did. Yes, I talked to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Shut up!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How did the golden snitch do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not well. Not with that face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can I use my resolve?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a saving throw, unfortunately. That's not a skill check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn it. 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11? This is to craft the--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm doing the whole kit and caboodle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The entire thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, if I can break it up into chunks, then that will be-- taking it apart. I'm going to take it apart, I'm going to put it back together, and I'm going to add the fifth barrel.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's been pretty soon. That was just a few days ago in Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, for the ammunition it has to be a little more specific. We'll get to that. With the time you have at this point, you've just managed to finish that before you guys are ready to go. Cool, all right. So what's the intent, guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So we're heading back to Emon. But we did send a letter ahead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You sent a letter, notifying them of what has happened, that Whitestone has been freed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And remember I said make sure to tell them how Vox Machina saved the day, and how wonderful we are, and you said, yes you would do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As far as you know, that's been sent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So all the messages-- because I sent word out to Gilmore, as well-- we haven't heard anything back from anyone, though. It's too soon, yeah?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's been pretty soon. That was just a few days ago in Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, for the ammunition it has to be a little more specific. We'll get to that. With the time you have at this point, you've just managed to finish that before you guys are ready to go. Cool, all right. So what's the intent, guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And remember I said make sure to tell them how Vox Machina saved the day, and how wonderful we are, and you said, yes you would do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As far as you know, that's been sent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So all the messages-- because I sent word out to Gilmore, as well-- we haven't heard anything back from anyone, though. It's too soon, yeah?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. I'd say you're looking at probably about five to six days' travel on horseback to get back to Emon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I also think I probably sent a letter to Pike to let her know that we're safe and we survived everything, and that she would've been at Winter's Crest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was hard thinking of an address in Vasselheim that would reach her, but you did your best to conjecture, and you attempted it, so we'll see if it finds its way.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Maybe it worked! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so how are you guys getting back to Emon?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: On our horses that we own. Do we own horses?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They were eaten by harpies.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go buy some horses.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: On the harpies that we own.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They've got to, right? They have horses here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are a couple liveries here that do sell, aside from keeping the horses of the actual guard.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Percival, you have the run of this place, somewhat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to assume that I can use some family connections to finagle a few horses.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You haven't seen Drez Vina in about a year and a half.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If anything goes wrong, we'll hear about it, and we can come back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's going on with the spinning black cube? Are we okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Isn't Allura on her way here? Aren't a lot of people on their way here? I'm so confused.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true, wasn't Allura on her way?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What happened in 2015? The whole way through. Start from the beginning.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, first there were dinosaurs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Last you were told, Allura is coming with a couple of companions to investigate the ziggurat. There was no indication that you're required for that, nor could you really aid in that--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Speak for yourself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, your vast understanding of arcane elements and the history of magic should come in handy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And Drez Vina, right? He's coming, too? He's had a notice to him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Drez Vina?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not Drez Vina.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's in the Wayback Machine.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: When you had heard the party was going to Whitestone, you were already in fear of what may lie there, knowing the history of Percy's past and hearing bits here and there. You came to try and catch them before they left, but by the time you came to Emon, they had already been gone for quite some time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pike!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hi!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What are you doing here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why are you sleeping outside? You have a bed upstairs!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, I do! I couldn't sleep!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I thought you were in Vasselheim!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long have you been here? So many questions!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, I've spent a few weeks in Draconia trying to secure aid for us in Whitestone."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I drop my invisibility and go over and give Tibsy a big hug.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Out of nowhere you, (startled grumbling) And pushes you away and grabs the staff and spins around.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's me! It's me! Little, yellow, different! Me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You mustn't sneak up on a Stormwind like that."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, I just, it's been so long and we've been through so much without you. I was overcome, I'm sorry. Friends, it's all right! Pike, I cleared the danger!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I go in. Hi Tibs!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys walk in, you see Tiberius seems both surprised and keeping a composure element to him, his chin high. "Greetings and salutations, friends. I'd hoped I'd see you before heading out."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Heading out?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Heading what?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Indeed I am. But over these past few weeks I've been continuously balked by my father. By the time I had given up and came to join you, word found me of your victory. Thus, I instead remained here in Emon. I've had some time to think, being here on my own for the better part of a week now. I consider you all friends and fine warriors, mostly. I've enjoyed our travels together, I've learned much, and for that I thank you sincerely. However, my diplomatic mission has fallen to the wayside in these recent months, and while my personal mission to recover these artifacts has grown stagnant, as proven by my time in Draconia, I have still yet to gain the respect of my family. I feel that the path of Vox Machina and the path of Tiberius Stormwind haven't been aligned for some time. In hearing about your victory with the Stormwinds-- oh, that's me-- your victory with the Briarwoods, made me think that while I was sure that you all needed me to protect you, I may have underestimated you all. Perhaps you are quite capable without a Stormwind. And perhaps I am more capable on my own pursuits, with no distractions. I respect you all very deeply, and hope to continue to hear of your exploits across Tal'Dorei, but I think it is time--" And he takes his staff and smacks it on the ground and picks up his bag of things. "I think it is time this dragonborn carved his own path."
